description_long: This object consists of a tall, narrow, stone obelisk set on a rectangular stone base, accompanied by a smaller, upright rectangular plaque or headstone. The obelisk is weathered, with visible discoloration and moss or lichen growth, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for a long period. The base is surrounded by a patch of grass, with the stonework partially embedded in the ground. The smaller plaque features a dark, inscribed panel, likely containing names, dates, or an epitaph, though the text is not fully legible. The overall structure is solemn and commemorative, typical of grave markers found in cemeteries or memorial parks. The materials and design indicate durability and a purpose of long-term remembrance.
description: A weathered stone obelisk and plaque set on a grassy base, serving as a grave marker with inscribed details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the obelisk and the inscribed plaque side by side, with the plaque's text panel visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the obelisk and the rectangular base, with both markers aligned.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of both the obelisk and the plaque, with less detail visible on the plaque.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the thinness of the obelisk and the alignment of the two markers on the base.
